page: Hetzner Cloud cax21 is an arm64-based virtual server belonging to the cax server family. It features 4 shared vCPUs powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via an 80 GB SSD, and there are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1805.19, and the multi-core score is 7235.85, both falling into the average category. Large block memory bandwidth is average, with read speeds at 38.95 GB/sec and write speeds at 25.37 GB/sec, though small block cached read bandwidth is weaker at 278.39 GB/sec. Geekbench testing shows weaker performance, with a single-core score of 1032.0 and a multi-core score of 3310.0. Cryptographic and image processing benchmarks are poor, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring 646.59 and Geekbench image processing scoring 1882.0.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cax21 offers a budget-friendly entry point into ARM-based cloud computing, utilizing shared CPU resources to maintain a low-cost profile. Due to its shared CPU allocation and modest memory bandwidth, it is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, development environments, simple database operations, and utility scripting rather than resource-intensive or high-throughput production applications.
